response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
Dealing with fake content online is a constant headache, especially for someone like me who's been using social media since the early days. I remember when it was just people sharing photos of their pets or funny videos; now it's充斥着谎言和阴谋论。As a self-employed carpenter in Los Angeles, I often look at these platforms to stay connected with friends and family, but sometimes that connection feels fake too. With my high neuroticism score, I tend to worry about things a lot—seeing those manipulated stories or deepfake videos can really get under my skin. It's not just about the technical aspects; it's emotional when you know something is untrue.

One thing that has helped me personally is learning to question every source before believing anything. My parents have contrasting educations; one with a PhD and the other without any formal schooling, so I've learned from them both to think critically regardless of their level. Even though my own education stopped at lower secondary, logic isn't something you can ignore.

I also try not to spend too much time on social media since it says I only use it a few times weekly anyway—it wouldn't be healthy for me otherwise given how much anxiety fake content causes. My hobbies like birdwatching and cinema help distract from this stress sometimes. And with my low extraversion, maybe that's why; I don't really need constant external validation or engagement.

But the problem extends beyond individuals—there needs to be more effort by social media platforms themselves if we're going to tackle this effectively.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I derived my opinion from personal experience as a long-time user with high neuroticism, which makes me sensitive to misinformation. I drew on critical thinking skills learned from parents with different educational backgrounds (PhD vs none) and contrasted that with my own lower secondary education level but still emphasized the importance of logic in discerning truth.
